splits.definition: Fix sales process to improve close rate via in-person closing before attempting pricing increases; process is the primary constraint, not leads or offer.
Higher price anchors (10x upsell) improve base offer conversion by resetting perceived value; conviction in the offer drives sales success.
Optimize for volume and proof before optimizing for close rate; premature close-rate optimization wastes proof-building opportunities.

definition: Optimize sales fundamentals: close rate via process, then pricing via anchoring, then volume.
rationale: Cluster conflates three distinct concepts: (1) process optimization mechanics, (2) pricing psychology, and (3) when to optimize which metric. Clean splits preserve load-bearing distinctions.

definition: Courage is acting despite fear; the interval between perceiving risk and taking action defines courage; action builds confidence retroactively.
rationale: Clean L0 concept—all members explore fear-action decoupling and the feedback loop between action and confidence. Proposed canonical captures the core.

definition: Educational content builds loyal, converting audiences; entertainment builds large, uncommitted audiences—they are distinct funnels, not conversion stages.
rationale: Post-Acquisition Integration ("keep platform separate via licensing") is a legal/operational detail, not a content strategy concept. Kick the outlier; rest cohere.

splits.definition: One trusted relationship with a peer inside the organization drives 5x longer tenure by anchoring belonging and identity.
Structure equity and ownership to prevent holdouts; batch commitments before fulfillment; manage transitions through shared ownership.
Alternate status/title bumps with perks to prevent clients or staff from accepting incentive then departing.

definition: Retention is driven by belonging (people), deal structure (ownership), and mixed incentives (perks + status).
rationale: Cluster spans three orthogonal retention mechanisms: psychological (belonging), legal (ownership structure), and tactical (perks mix). Each deserves its own L0.

definition: Target wealthy, high-intent prospects via qualification friction and lead scoring; right avatar + close match drives high-ticket conversion; wrong avatar guarantees failure.
rationale: All members reinforce same L0 principle: qualification > volume. Friction, scoring, and avatar targeting are tactics serving the same concept. Centroid name is canonical.

definition: Strong brand enables premium pricing—higher prices trigger virtuous cycle of better margins, talent, reputation, and demand—until willingness to pay stops.
rationale: Tight cluster on brand-pricing-margin compounding. All members articulate the same feedback loop from different angles. Proposed canonical captures the umbrella concept.

definition: Insurance mandates (Medicare Part B at 65+) create predictable high-volume lead generation; broker commission structure misaligns incentives (keeping overcharge vs saving money).
rationale: Centroid name "Deal Structure Analysis" is too generic. The real L0 is how insurance regulation and industry economics create a lead-gen moat. Rename to capture the actual insight.

definition: Continuity: after initial program phase, upsell renewal or higher-tier continuation with guarantee, leveraging momentum and proof from first stage.
rationale: Core cluster is about upsell timing and structure. Kicked members are about distribution, launch sequencing, and general revenue mechanics—orthogonal to continuity. Tighten the concept.

definition: High margins (50%+) enable reinvestment and faster growth; 50% margin business at $20M outperforms 10% margin business at $100M on compounding velocity.
rationale: Kicked members are about capital deployment (fund math, reinvestment loops), not margin generation. Centroid "Margins" conflates business profitability with financial leverage. Narrow to pure margin economics.

splits.definition: Compensation and work design should be outcome-based (results regardless of hours), separating maker (deep work) from manager (meetings) modes.
Each location needs independent P&L ownership with dedicated ops manager; decentralization enables accountability without top-down micromanagement.
High performance requires supportive personal life; most daily work supports a small passion core—accept this 95/5 split to avoid burnout.

definition: Outcome-based work enables discretionary effort; organizational design (decentralization + support) unlocks it.
rationale: Cluster spans work design, organizational structure, and team psychology. Three distinct levers: compensation model, reporting structure, and leader enablement.

definition: What happens after behavior determines if it repeats; consistent behavioral reinforcement hardens into identity; language and say-do consistency compound compliance.
rationale: All members articulate behavioral psychology: stimulus-response-reinforcement chains shape identity and future behavior. Centroid name is slightly wonky but members cohere tightly—accept with alias suggestion.

definition: Replace role-based hiring with workflow decomposition; map granular tasks across roles, then automate task-by-task; admin workflows are automation-ready now.
rationale: Tight L0—all members articulate the same operational shift: from hiring for roles to automating workflows. Centroid is canonical; strong cluster.

definition: Pricing communicates quality and filters audiences; establish emotional context and perceived value before revealing price; set fixed price upfront to avoid renegotiation.
rationale: Centroid focuses narrowly on the anchoring tactic. The real L0 is broader: how price communication (language, timing, anchors, signals) shapes buyer psychology and market selection. Rename to capture the umbrella.

definition: Motivation source (pain, shame, meaning) doesn't matter—results do; passion is external fickle; purpose (why/how) is internal persistent; meaning justifies suffering.
rationale: Coherent L0 on intrinsic motivation: all members explore how to tap emotional fuel (negative or positive), connect to larger purpose, and sustain effort through difficulty. Centroid captures the cluster.

definition: Scale ad spend after landing page and creative are optimized; maintain ROAS by improving quality (creative) before increasing quantity (budget); measurement is prerequisite.
rationale: Centroid "Ad Spend Scaling" is directional. Broader L0 is optimization methodology: measure first, optimize creative, then scale spend. Rename to capture the causal chain.
